name: Satakunta (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the azure chief of the Satakunta arms
the golden base of the arms and the ducal crown
the silver sword and the two stars

description: A swallow-tailed flag divided per fess blue over yellow, charged with a crowned black bear brandishing a silver sword, flanked by two silver seven-pointed stars in the blue chief — the banner of the Satakunta coat of arms.
history: Satakunta became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic arms of the Satakunta province documented from the late sixteenth century.

features.role: two silver seven-pointed stars flanking the bear in the blue chief
a crowned black bear brandishing a silver sword — the bear of Satakunta
features.symbolism: the wild forests of Satakunta; the bear as 'king of the forest' in Finnish tradition
